creator: zarychtam@plan-b.pwste.edu.pl
text:
(In reply to Mikhail T. from comment #10)

>That's exactly, how it works, Marek, in any organization: people, who make a mistake,
>get an earful -- which reduces their chance of reoffending.
It wasn't any mistake, everything was planned and announced in advance by bofh@. FreeBSD software support model differs a lot from the one required by ownCloud. I really do appreciate this model and think it's one of the strengths of using FreeBSD. One can install either the most recent version of the software or any other stable version, but there is no place for security risk and maintaining deprecated software in the ports tree. If the port committers act the way you demand, not purging some ports on time, and giving the illusion of backporting security patches to the software which was EOL upstream it would be definitely a security threat and abuse of users' trust. Such practices should be avoided.

>I don't blame anyone for the non-realizing -- the port is still giving a mistaken
>impression of being usable with any PHP version instead of insisting on 7.4.
I really wonder why sunopet@ hasn't deprecated this port yet due to missing dependencies. It's high time to make this port at least broken, especially since manual installation could be done with ease.

>Frankly, I'd rather the trigger-happy busy bodies "migrated to Linux" instead.
Thank you for evaluating FreeBSD and for your feedback.
